political.s: Color Health spent $60,000 on federal lobbying in 2022. The company engages in government relations primarily around healthcare policy issues. As a private healthcare technology company, their political engagement appears limited compared to larger health insurers.

charity.s: Color Health partners with nonprofit organizations including the American Cancer Society to address cancer screening and prevention. The company has not disclosed comprehensive charitable giving totals or annual donation amounts.
charity.focus: Cancer prevention, screening, and equity in healthcare access

environment.s: Not applicable - Color Health is a digital health technology and genetic testing company without significant direct environmental operations.

labor.s: Based on Glassdoor data, Color Health employees report mixed satisfaction with compensation (3.9/5 stars). Average salaries range from approximately $43,000-$167,000 depending on role. No major OSHA or NLRB violations reported. Employees cite good benefits and work-life balance but some mention past layoffs.

dei.s: Color Health demonstrates pro-DEI positioning through partnerships with organizations serving communities of color, including the Cleveland African American Prostate Cancer Project. The company explicitly mentions commitment to accessible care for diverse populations. CEO and leadership communications emphasize health equity.

animals.s: Not applicable - Color Health provides genetic testing and virtual cancer care services; no animal testing is involved in their operations.

guns.s: Not applicable - Color Health is a healthcare technology company with no connection to firearms manufacturing or sales.

privacy.s: Color Health maintains HIPAA compliance and has published comprehensive privacy policies covering consumer health data and protected health information. The company commits to HIPAA-compliant data protection and implements safeguards for genetic information. Data sharing is limited to authorized purposes with patient consent.

execPay.s: Executive compensation data not publicly disclosed for private company. Average employee compensation ranges $43,000-$167,000 by role. Limited data available on CEO-to-worker pay ratios or executive compensation details.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
